text.title: embedded-scratchpad-field Failure Modes
text.summary: Five failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Scratchpad/reasoning field placed AFTER the answer (post-hoc rationalisation, not CoT).
antipattern.reason: Autoregressive generation means the answer was committed before reasoning was produced. The reasoning field becomes a story explaining a decision already made, not the chain that produced it.

section.p: Symptom: reasoning frequently contradicts the answer; CoT lift is zero or negative. Root cause: schema field order. Fix: swap to place scratchpad first.

antipattern.description: `reasoning` field with empty or generic description ("think step by step").
antipattern.reason: Without a concrete description naming WHAT to think about, the model writes filler. The lift collapses; tokens are wasted.

section.p: Symptom: scratchpad content is generic prose unrelated to the input. Root cause: missing description anchor. Fix: rewrite description to name the dependent answer fields and the criteria to consider; cap word count.

antipattern.description: Multiple competing scratchpad fields in one schema (`reasoning` + `analysis` + `notes`).
antipattern.reason: The model splits effort across the fields, none of which gets enough budget; the prompt becomes ambiguous about which should drive the answer.

section.p: Symptom: every scratchpad field is short and shallow. Root cause: schema accumulation over iterations. Fix: keep exactly one scratchpad per schema; if you need multi-stage thinking, split into two SO calls.

antipattern.description: Scratchpad that just paraphrases the input instead of reasoning about it.
antipattern.reason: Description does not demand analysis; model defaults to safe paraphrase, gaining no insight.

section.p: Symptom: scratchpad reads like a TL;DR of the input. Root cause: vague description. Fix: description must instruct the model to compare, evaluate, or decide — not summarise.

antipattern.description: `max_tokens` cap or word-budget so tight the scratchpad cannot breathe.
antipattern.reason: For non-trivial reasoning, ~200-500 tokens is needed; smaller caps truncate the chain mid-step and the answer suffers.

section.p: Symptom: scratchpad truncated; answer wrong on hard cases. Root cause: over-aggressive token budget. Fix: cap at ~300 words for free-form, ~5-10 items for list-style; measure on an eval and adjust.
